metadata.title: Vote FEI 2026 | Mucha & Buček
metadata.description: Campaign page of Tomáš Mucha and Martin Buček for the FEI VŠB-TUO Academic Senate elections 2026–2029. Send us your idea — we want to listen!
metadata.og_description: Mucha & Buček — for a transparent, accessible FEI VŠB-TUO Academic Senate 2026–2029

hero.subline: Two FEI students with a real plan for the Academic Senate.
hero.subline2: Tell us what bothers you — we'll act on it.
hero.voting_ended: Thank you for participating in the 2026 elections

about.label: Candidates
about.heading: Who are we?
about.subline: Two FEI students who want to be a genuine voice of the academic community in the senate.
about.bio_mucha: I study at FEI and believe every student deserves to be heard. I care about transparency in academia and modernising student-facing systems.
about.why_mucha: I want student voices to reach where decisions are made — on budgets, rules and the faculty's future — not just during election campaigns, but every day.
about.bio_bucek: I study at FEI and believe that good communication between management and students is the foundation of quality education. I want to be the link that delivers concrete results.
about.why_bucek: The senate approves the budget, rules, and elects the dean. That's exactly the place where we can systematically improve things — not just make promises.
about.award_bucek: Best Lecturer at VŠB — voted by students

program.label: Our programme
program.heading: What do we promise?
program.subline: Only realistic commitments within the senate's legal powers.
program.cube_label: 4 pillars of our programme — interactive cube
program.footer_note: All commitments are within the legally defined competencies of the Academic Senate (Act No. 111/1998 Coll.)
program.p1_desc: Every month we'll publish a clear summary of senate decisions. No bureaucracy — just plain information for everyone.
program.p1_detail: Every month we'll publish a report on senate decisions — including votes and our positions. No secret deals. Available on this website 24/7.
program.p2_desc: When approving the faculty budget, we'll actively push for student priorities — better equipment, accessible spaces.
program.p2_detail: At every budget vote, we'll actively push for allocation to student-proposed projects — labs, equipment, digital tools.
program.p3_desc: This website will remain active throughout the entire 2026–2029 term.
program.p3_detail: This website will stay active for the entire 2026–2029 term. We'll publish reports, suggestions, and results of our senate work.
program.p4_desc: We'll initiate a revision of exam regulations, focusing on transparent grading and fair retake policies.
program.p4_detail: We'll initiate a formal proposal to revise exam regulations — especially retake rules, transparent grading, and clear criteria.
program.p5_desc: We'll support modernisation of student-facing systems — from portals to e-learning platforms.
program.p5_detail: We'll push for upgrades to digital tools — from EDISON to e-learning platforms. Specific proposals will be based on your suggestions.
program.p6_desc: Every semester we'll hold a public meeting where we answer questions and discuss what's happening at FEI.
program.p6_detail: Every semester we'll organise an open meeting with students — where you can ask anything about senate activity. First one within 3 months of election.

vote.label: How to vote
vote.heading: Your vote is a
vote.subline: Elections take place electronically May 12–15, 2026 via the UNIS system. It takes 2 minutes.
vote.step1_desc: Via the UNIS VŠB-TUO system using your FEI login credentials.
vote.step2_desc: Find Mucha & Buček in the FEI candidate list.
vote.step3_desc: Anonymous voting. The whole process takes 2 minutes.
vote.cta_desc: Electronically via UNIS VŠB-TUO. Every FEI student has one vote. Use it — for people who will listen.

faq.label: Questions & answers
faq.heading: Have questions?
faq.subline: We'll answer the most common ones. If you can't find what you're looking for —
faq.q1: What can a senator actually influence?
faq.a1: The FEI Academic Senate has legally defined powers: it approves the faculty budget, elects the dean, approves internal regulations (including exam rules), decides on opening and closing study programmes, and oversees academic freedom. This is not a ceremonial role — these are real decision-making powers.
faq.q2: How does the voting process work?
faq.a2: Voting takes place electronically via the UNIS VŠB-TUO system from May 12–15, 2026. Just log in with your FEI credentials. Voting takes literally 2 minutes and is anonymous.
faq.a3: The FEI Senate has 5 student seats out of 13 total members. A stronger joint programme and coordinated voting in the senate gives us realistically better chances of pushing through change. Two well-informed senators can more effectively gather feedback from different student groups.
faq.q4: What happens with suggestions submitted through this website?
faq.a4: We read every approved suggestion personally. Suggestions will serve as the basis for our senate priorities — specific topics we'll focus on when voting on budgets, regulations and other matters. After election we'll publish our first report with an overview of received suggestions and our plan.
faq.q5: Why should I care about senate elections?
faq.a5: The senate directly affects things you experience every day — exam rules, funding allocation for lab equipment, study conditions. Three years with a passive senate or with active representatives makes a big difference. And voting takes 2 minutes.
faq.q6: When will election results be published?
faq.a6: The FEI election commission will publish results immediately after voting closes (after May 15, 2026). We'll publish our statement and next steps on this website.

suggestions.label: Suggestions
suggestions.heading: What do you want to change?
suggestions.subline: Send us an idea, complaint or proposal. We read every suggestion personally and turn it into concrete steps in the senate.
suggestions.form_text_placeholder: What would you like to change or improve at FEI?
suggestions.form_footer: Suggestions go through moderation before publishing. Personal details are optional.

privacy.heading: Privacy policy
privacy.s1_text: This website is part of the election campaign of Tomáš Mucha and Martin Buček for the FEI VŠB-TUO Academic Senate (May 2026). Contact:
privacy.s2_text: When submitting suggestions and voting in polls, we hash IP addresses (one-way transformation) solely to prevent abuse. The original IP address is not stored in readable form. The hash is retained only as long as necessary for protection purposes and is not shared with third parties.
privacy.s3_text: If you subscribe to our newsletter, we store only your email address. It is used solely to send information about the Vote FEI 2026 campaign and is not provided to any third parties. You can unsubscribe at any time by replying to any campaign email.
privacy.s4_text: Suggestions submitted through the form are stored in a database and published on the site after moderator approval. Your name is optional — if you leave it blank, the suggestion will be published anonymously. Suggestion content is not shared with third parties.
privacy.s5_text: This site does not use analytical or advertising cookies. The only locally stored data is technical (e.g. information about submitting a poll vote), stored in your browser's localStorage.
privacy.s6_text: If you have questions about the processing of personal data, contact us at
